LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RYAN ALLIS AND HIVE GLOBAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ADAM PUMM IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PARAGRAPHS 16, 43-45, AND 182-183 AND THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COUNSEL FOR MR. ALLIS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR THE COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William Bowen, Esq. 213-761-4332 for Plaintiff Adam Pumm through Court Call.
Kristin Iversen, Esq. 415-788-1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for DEFENDANT NEIL BAGCHI.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues delayed discovery. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentatvie ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEIL BAGCHI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Attorney Bagchi's statute-of-limitations demurrer under CCP 340.6 is OVERRULED; the complaint adequately pleads delayed discovery. Bagchi's demurrer to the third cause of action (fraud) on specificity grounds is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Finally, causes of action three, seven and eight are effectively based on a theory that attorney Bagchi conspired with defendant Allis; thus, prior court approval is required under Civil Code 1714.10. The exception in 1714.10(c) does not apply, as the complaint (16:13-14) pleads that "Bagchi was representing the partnership," and no "independent legal duty to the plaintiff" is pled. Hence, this third demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RYAN ALLIS, HIVE GLOBAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ADAM PUMM.

Defendants Ryan Allis and Hive Global, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of the complaint filed by plaintiff Adam Pumm is granted without leave to amend as to paragraphs 16, 43-45, and 182-183 and the request for attorney's fees and denied as to request for punitive damages. The allegations regarding drug use and romantic interludes are irrelevant to Mr. Pumm's claims. The tort claims alleged by Mr. Pumm adequately support the request for punitive damages. The complaint does not cite a statutory, contractual, or other legal basis for the request for fees. Mr. Pumm's argument that he might be able to recover fees under Prentice v. N. Am. Title Guar. Corp. (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618 lacks merit because the tort of another doctrine allows for recovery of fees as damages. Ms. Pumm's argument that he might be able to recover fees under Civil Code 3264.6 because Mr. Allis possibly violated the Uniform Trade Secrets Act fails as no such claim is currently alleged.

Counsel for Mr. Allis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RYAN ALLIS, HIVE GLOBAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY ADAM PUMM.

Defendants Ryan Allis and Hive Global, Inc.'s demurrer to the first through fifth and seventh and eighth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Adam Pumm is overruled in its entirety. Mr. Pumm adequately alleges a claim for breach of an oral partnership agreement and Mr. Allis raises disputed factual issues not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The complaint alleges the existence of a 50/50 partnership and those allegations are accepted as true on a demurrer. Mr. Allis acknowledges that a partner can also be an employee. The offer letter, which is not attached to the complaint but provided by Mr. Allis, is not a fully integrated agreement that controls the alleged partnership. Mr. Allis' statute of limitations argument lacks merit on this demurrer. Mr. Allis abandoned his argument that the claim accrued when the contract was formed. Even if the court entertained the new reply argument, paragraphs 136-138 do not conclusively show when the breach occurred. As to the breach of fiduciary duty claim, there are disputed facts whether the parties created a partnership and a partnership relationship can support a fiduciary relationship. The new arguments in the reply are procedurally improper and fail at any rate. The allegations of breach are factually sufficient and refer to issues presumptively within Mr. Allis' knowledge. As to the fraud claim, Mr. Allis misreads the complaint. Paragraph 216 clearly states that the basis for the claim was the misrepresentation regarding Mr. Bagchi. Paragraphs 96 and 216 allege when and how the misrepresentation occurred. The allegations of a 50/50 partnership are sufficient to support the promissory estoppel claim. The demurrer to the unfair business practices cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint sufficiently alleges an unfair and fraudulent business practice. As to the interference claims, Mr. Pumm sufficiently alleges that Mr. Allis interfered with Mr. Pumm's ability to obtain money from the Hive conference attendees.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
